MV OCEANBEAUTY
===============
TYPE  : SINGLE DECK BULK CARRIER
BUILT : JAN 2013
YARD  : COSCO ZHOUSHAN, CHINA
FLAG  : LIBERIA
PORT  : MONROVIA
CLASS : CLASS : RINA BULK CARRIER ESP-CSR-BC-A
               ALLOWED COMBINATION OF SPECIFIED EMPTY HOLDS
L O A : 189.99 M
DEPTH : 18.00 M
BEAM  : 32.26 M
SUMMER DWT/DFT : ABT 56,803.9 MT AT ABT 12.80 M
INT'L GT / NT  : 33,049 / 19,132
HOLDS/HATCHES  : 5 / 5
HATCH COVERS   : CYLINDER FOLDING TYPE
GRAIN CAPACITY : 2,529,722.1 CFT
-VSL IS GRAB / GRAIN / EVAPORATOR FITTED / AHL FITTED.
-VSL IS SUITABLE FOR GRAB DISCHARGE / GRAB FITTED.
G E A R : CRANES 4 SETS X 30 MT (HOOK CAPACITY)
CARGO LIFTING CAPACITY WITH GRABS : 24 MT
GRABS CAPACITY : 12.0 / 10.0 / 8.5 / 7.0 / 5.5 CBM
MAX CRANE OUTREACH : ABT 11.50 M

SPEED / CONSUMPTION
-------------------
AT SEA:
BASED ON CLEAN BOTTOM, GOOD WEATHER CONDITIONS WHICH MEANS WINDS UP TO
AND INCL. BEAUFORT SCALE 4 / DOUGLAS WIND SEA STATE 3 (COMBINED SIGNIFICANT
WAVE HEIGHT 1.25M) AND IN DEEP WATERS, NO  ADVERSE CURRENTS, NO NEGATIVE
INFLUENCE OF SWELL, PASSAGE WITH UNRESTRICTED VISIBILITY, NOT CONGESTED
WATERS, STRAITS, NARROWED WATERS WHERE VESSEL NORMALLY TRANSITS WITH REDUCED
SPEED AND NOT DURING MANEUVERING. "ABT" MEANS 0.5 KN DECREASE IN SPEED AND
5% INCREASE IN CONSUMPTION.

BALLAST : ABT 14.00 KN ON/ABT 27.5 MT VLSFO 380CST + ABT 0.1 MT LSMGO
LADEN   : ABT 13.50 KN ON/ABT 28.0 MT VLSFO 380CST + ABT 0.1 MT LSMGO
ECO (B) : ABT 12.50 KN ON/ABT 22.5 MT VLSFO 380CST + ABT 0.1 MT LSMGO
ECO (L) : ABT 12.00 KN ON/ABT 22.5 MT VLSFO 380CST + ABT 0.1 MT LSMGO
-IN PORT:
I D L E   : ABT 3.0 MT VLSFO (380CST) + ABT 0.1 MT LSMGO (DMA)
GEAR WORK : ABT 3.5 - 4.5 MT VLSFO (380CST) DEPENDING ON NUMBER OF
CRANES WORKING + ABT 0.1 MT LSMGO (DMA)

BUNKER GRADES (AS PER ISO 8217-E 2017) : VLSFO 380CST (RMG380) / LSMGO DMA
VESSEL'S ABOVE DESCRIBED PERFORMANCE APPLIES ONLY WHEN VESSEL
BURNS IN MAIN ENGINE VLSFO 380CST.
VSL HAS LIBERTY TO BURN LSMGO FOR MAIN ENGINE WHEN MANEUVERING
IN SHALLOW / NARROW / RESTRICTED WATER , CANAL RIVERS OR IN AND OUT OF
PORTS.
NO DECK CARGO TO BE LOADED
HATCH DIMENSIONS
----------------
NO.1  18.86 X 18.26 M
NO.2  21.32 X 18.26 M
NO.3  21.32 X 18.26 M
NO.4  21.32 X 18.26 M
NO.5  21.32 X 18.26 M
STRENGTHS
---------
TANK TOP : HOLDS NO.1-3-5 = 25 MT/M2
TANK TOP : HOLDS NO.2 - 4 = 20 MT/M2
DECK     : N/A
GRAIN BREAKDOWN
---------------
HO.1 =  459,443.12 CFT
HO.2 =  541,493.72 CFT
HO.3 =  513,942.02 CFT
HO.4 = 541,494.43 CFT
HO.5 =  473,384.86 CFT
----------------------
TTL = 2,529,758.15 CFT
IF BUNKER GRADES ISO 8217 - E 2017 IS NOT AVAILABLE CHRTRS MAY USE ISO
8217-E 2010
BALLAST CLAUSE FOR LOW SALINITY WATERS
1. BALLASTING TIME (FULL BALLAST EXCEPT C/HOLD NO.3): 22 HOURS (AT ABOUT 750
MT/HOUR).
DE-BALLASTING (FULL BALLAST EXCEPT C/HOLD NO.3): 15 HOURS (AT ABOUT 1,100
MT/HOUR) - EXCLUDING STRIPPING.
2. BALLASTING IN LOW SALINITY WATERS (BELOW 0.9 PSU).
THE APT IS USED AS "FRESH WATER MODULE" OPERATION WHEN BALLASTING IN A FRESH
WATER PORT (SALINITY BELOW 0.9 PSU).
SHIP'S APT HAS TO RETAIN HIGH SALINITY WATER (BALLAST) SO TO ENABLE THE
SYSTEM NEUTRALIZE THE LOW SALINITY INTAKE BALLAST.
THIS WILL NEED TO BE KNOWN BEFORE LOADING HER CARGO, SINCE INEVITABLY IT MAY
LIMIT HER CARGO INTAKE AND AFFECT HER TRIM, DRAFT,     STABILITY, DRAFT
LIMITATIONS ETC. AT DISCHARGING PORT.
THE QUANTITY OF HIGH SALINITY BALLAST IN APT DEPENDS ON THE PSU OF THE LOW
SALINITY INTAKE BALLAST (AT DISCHARGING PORT).
THIS BALLAST IN APT COULD BECOME ZERO IF THE TRADE OF THE VESSEL GUARANTEES
THAT THE WATER AT THE DISCHARGING PORT IS OF SALINITY OVER 0.9 PSU. SO WHEN
THE VESSEL DISCHARGES HER CARGO AND TAKES BALLAST (AT WATER SALINITY OVER
0.9 PSU), THERE WILL BE NO NEED TO USE THE "FRESH WATER MODULE" OPERATION
AND SO APT CAN BE EMPTY.
SHOULD THE ABOVE WILL INCUR, OWNERS TO BE KEPT HARMLESS OF ANY LIABILITY
UNDER THE C/P WARRANTIES/CONDITIONS/DESCRIPTION IN RESPECT THEREOF
ADA
